plan_adherence_notes: The decomposition flow executed correctly: questions phase identified 5 independent subproblems and spawned children, children each ran full question→research→plan→implement cycles building all 5 features (ForceDirectedGraph, CptPanel, useAnimationPropagation, networkExport/Import, HistoryContext+undo/redo). Parent research then correctly identified all features existed and pivoted to a 6-step integration plan. All 6 parent integration steps completed faithfully: (1) selectedMarketId state + onNodeClick wiring in MarketDetail, (2) animation propagation integrated into ForceDirectedGraph, (3) GraphToolbar created with export/import/view-toggle, (4) GraphToolbar integrated into MarketDetail with cache invalidation on import, (5) ConnectedForceGraph bridge component wiring AssumptionContext to ForceDirectedGraph, (6) BayesNetGraph.test.tsx fixed with AssumptionProvider wrapper + new GraphIntegration.test.tsx added. One functional gap: useProbabilityEdit (hooks.ts:159-162) does not invalidate queryKeys.marketCpt() after edits, so CptPanel shows stale data after probability changes — this was flagged by child-2 validation but not addressed by the parent integration.
research_quality_notes: All 8 questions answered with confidence >= 0.85, each citing specific file:line evidence. The critical insight — that all 5 features already existed from child task execution — was correctly identified, enabling a dramatically reduced integration-only plan (440 estimated lines vs original 1500-2500). Assumption checks were accurate: a1 correctly marked WRONG (coexistence vs replacement), all others CORRECT. The research-to-plan pipeline was efficient, with decisions d1-d6 directly traceable to research answers. Child task research was also strong, particularly child-1's discovery that no directed-edge API existed (shaping the clique co-membership edge derivation approach).
question_completeness_notes: 8 questions covered the core design space well: replacement vs coexistence strategy, CPT editing scope, animation approach, export format, undo/redo scope, D3 dependency philosophy, animation state management, network size expectations. The decomposition into 5 independent child tasks was appropriate and correctly identified. However, several important concerns were not raised during the question phase.
missed_questions: How will adding new React context dependencies (useAssumptions) to existing components affect their test suites? — This omission directly caused 13 BayesNetGraph test regressions across 4 of 5 child tasks, which the parent had to fix in step 6.

After CPT probability edits, how does the CPT query cache get invalidated? useProbabilityEdit only invalidates market and account queries, not the CPT query — this is a functional bug that persists in the final implementation.
Accessibility: No consideration of keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, screen reader support, or prefers-reduced-motion for the interactive SVG graph, drag/zoom, or animation system.
Mobile/touch interaction: d3-zoom and d3-drag have touch support but UX differs significantly from mouse — no design consideration for responsive or touch use.

Vitest path alias resolution: The existing test infrastructure has a pre-existing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND issue with @/ imports that prevents ALL tests from running — this was not identified during research and means no tests could actually be verified at runtime.

test_results.typescript_compilation: Clean — only 2 unused-import warnings in test file (TS6133)
test_results.vitest: ALL 39 test files fail with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for @/ path aliases — this is a PRE-EXISTING infrastructure issue confirmed by testing on main branch, NOT a regression from this branch
test_results.note: 8 new test files created with comprehensive coverage. TypeScript confirms they compile. Cannot verify runtime pass/fail due to pre-existing vitest config issue.

child_task_summary.child_1_force_graph: approve — D3 force-directed graph with full interactivity. Minor gaps: BayesNetGraph not deleted, onNodeClick not wired (parent fixed the latter).
child_task_summary.child_2_cpt_panel: changes_requested — CPT editor implemented correctly but cache invalidation missing (queryKeys.marketCpt not invalidated after edits). Bug persists in final code.
child_task_summary.child_3_animation: changes_requested — BFS propagation animation implemented well but broke BayesNetGraph tests by adding useAssumptions dependency. Parent step 6 fixed the test regression.
child_task_summary.child_4_export_import: changes_requested — Export/import feature correct but scope creep (animation code added). Tests broken by useAssumptions. Parent step 6 fixed tests.
child_task_summary.child_5_undo_redo: changes_requested — Undo/redo with command pattern fully implemented. Same test regression as others. Parent step 6 fixed.

reason: The implementation delivers all 5 requested features: D3 force-directed graph with drag/zoom, interactive CPT editing via node click, evidence propagation animation with BFS simulation, network JSON export/import, and undo/redo with command pattern + keyboard shortcuts. The decomposition strategy was sound — 5 independent children built features in parallel, parent integrated them with a clean 6-step plan. TypeScript compiles clean. ~7,500 lines of well-structured code across 50+ files with 8 new test files. The parent integration correctly fixed the test regression (BayesNetGraph + AssumptionProvider) that 4/5 children introduced. Two residual issues exist but neither is blocking: (1) useProbabilityEdit does not invalidate the CPT query cache — a minor bug fixable by adding one line to hooks.ts:161, and (2) vitest module resolution is pre-existing broken infrastructure unrelated to this branch. Architecture decisions are sound: component coexistence over replacement, local-only undo/redo, hybrid JS+CSS animation, custom JSON schema over external formats. Approving with recommendation to fix CPT cache invalidation and vitest config in follow-up.
